title: Explicit Euler on the scalar linear test equation gives the first-order update

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the scalar linear test equation y prime equals a y, one explicit Euler step equals (1 + dt * a) times y.
informal.latex: For \(y\prime=a y\), explicit Euler gives \(y_+=y+\Delta t\,a y=(1+\Delta t\,a)y\).
assumptions: dt, a, and y are real numbers
the right-hand side value is a * y
informal.expected_conclusion: The explicit Euler linear-test step is multiplication by 1 + dt * a.

declarations.name: explicitEulerStep
declarations.statement: def explicitEulerStep (dt y f : Real) : Real := y + dt * f

declarations.name: explicitEulerLinearStep
declarations.statement: def explicitEulerLinearStep (dt a y : Real) : Real := explicitEulerStep dt y (a * y)

declarations.name: explicitEulerLinearStep_eq
declarations.statement: theorem explicitEulerLinearStep_eq (dt a y : Real) : explicitEulerLinearStep dt a y = (1 + dt * a) * y

tasks.input: Translate the explicit Euler linear-test-equation update identity into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem explicitEulerLinearStep_eq (dt a y : Real) : explicitEulerLinearStep dt a y = (1 + dt * a) * y

tasks.input: Given the explicit Euler definitions, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding explicitEulerLinearStep and explicitEulerStep, then using ring.
